mappings.reason: Establishing a data management process that addresses data retention limits provides the governance framework required to define and enforce the credit card data storage restriction.
Enforcing data retention with minimum and maximum timelines directly implements the storage restriction by mandating a retention limit of zero for prohibited credit card data elements.
Securely disposing of data according to sensitivity enables the non-storage requirement by ensuring payment data is removed after processing is complete.
Deploying a DLP solution to identify all sensitive data stored supports compliance verification by detecting any prohibited credit card data that may be retained.
Training workforce members on how to properly store sensitive data ensures personnel understand and execute the restriction against storing credit card information.
